description: Weekly, grouped dependency updates so a fresh CVE against a pinned transitive dep doesn't have to turn CI red before we notice (issue #59; see CLAUDE.md's Trivy CVE-response section). Groups mirror how upstream actually releases these families, so one PR = one coherent bump instead of a pile of single-package PRs.

vulnerabilityAlerts.description: Security fixes must not wait for Monday - open them the moment OSV/GHSA data says a pinned version is vulnerable. This is what turns the Trivy CI gate back into a backstop instead of the trigger.

packageRules.description: GitHub Actions pins in .github/workflows/*.yaml - one PR per week for all of them.
golang.org/x/* std-adjacent packages (x/net, x/sys, x/crypto, x/text, ...) release together upstream - batch them into one PR instead of one-per-package.
grpc-go + OpenTelemetry + genproto are versioned/released in lockstep upstream and tend to break the build if bumped independently.

k8s.io/* and sigs.k8s.io/* (client-go, apimachinery, controller-runtime, ...) - only minor/patch bumps are auto-groupable; they must stay on the k8s 1.35 / controller-runtime v0.23 line this operator is pinned to (see operator/go.mod).
Hold back k8s.io/*, sigs.k8s.io/* major bumps - they require a coordinated, manual controller-runtime upgrade, not an unattended PR.
Group Go indirect deps not covered by a more specific rule above into a single PR.
kind's node image, tracked via the custom regex manager below (Makefile's KIND_VERSION default and each workflow's KIND_NODE_IMAGE env var).

customManagers.description: kind node image version pinned in the top-level Makefile (KIND_VERSION) and in .github/workflows/*.yaml (KIND_NODE_IMAGE).
